meta.title: Prospect-to-Issue
meta.catalog: Guidewire PolicyCenter — New Business & Renewal Underwriting Catalog

steps.output: Enquiry logged in CRM; prospect record created if new
Product suitability confirmed; information gathered per product checklist
Application ID generated; data validated for completeness
Quote valid 30 days; reference logged in CRM

steps.activity: Application submitted to underwriting rules engine

steps.output: Risk assessed within 2 days (personal) / 5 days (commercial); info requested if needed
Accept standard / accept substandard / decline; recorded in PAS
Acceptance letter or renewal notice; payment instructions included

steps.activity: Prospect accepts terms and pays premium
steps.output: Payment received; acceptance confirmed in CRM
Policy number assigned; coverage start date confirmed
Schedule, certificate and PDS sent within 2 business days
Commission statement generated; confirmation sent to producing broker

swim_phases.name: Prospect qualification & quote
swim_phases.subtitle: Steps 1–4 · enquiry to quote
swim_phases.note: Prospects are qualified against a product suitability checklist before a 30-day quote is issued.

swim_phases.name: Underwriting assessment
swim_phases.subtitle: Steps 5–7 · rules, referral, decision
swim_phases.note: Only referred applications reach an underwriter; non-standard risks are priced with Actuarial before a decision.

swim_phases.name: Acceptance & issuance
swim_phases.subtitle: Steps 8–12 · terms to fulfilment
swim_phases.note: On acceptance and payment the policy issues, documents dispatch within two days, and producers are notified.

fitgap.summary_line: Overall alignment of SOP-INS-004 against the Guidewire PolicyCenter underwriting catalog

items.name: Multi-channel prospect intake
items.detail: Enquiry capture and CRM prospect creation across channels align with PolicyCenter submission intake.

items.name: Needs assessment & suitability
items.detail: A product checklist is used, but suitability is not captured as a structured, auditable needs analysis as the catalog recommends for conduct compliance.

items.name: Real-time rating & quote
items.detail: Rating-engine quote with 30-day validity aligns with PolicyCenter quoting.

items.name: Third-party risk-data enrichment
items.detail: Quotes use applicant-supplied data only. PolicyCenter enriches submissions with third-party risk data (property, flood, credit, prior-claims) for pricing accuracy and anti-selection control.

items.name: Auto-accept rules engine
items.detail: Rules-based auto-accept present, but no predictive risk-model score to widen straight-through processing.

items.name: KYC / sanctions screening
items.detail: No KYC or sanctions/PEP screening at new business. PolicyCenter screens applicants before binding.

items.name: Underwriter referral & SLAs
items.detail: Referral with 2-day/5-day SLAs and additional-info handling aligns with PolicyCenter referral workflow.

items.name: Substandard terms & actuarial pricing
items.detail: Accept/substandard/decline with actuarial pricing for non-standard risk aligns with PolicyCenter decisioning.

items.name: Reinsurance / facultative referral
items.detail: No facultative reinsurance referral or treaty-capacity check for large commercial risks above retention — a capacity and accumulation-control gap vs the catalog.

items.name: Terms communication & acceptance
items.detail: Acceptance letter / renewal notice with payment instructions aligns with PolicyCenter offer handling.

items.name: Disclosure & e-signature
items.detail: Acceptance is confirmed in CRM with no auditable e-signature on terms/PDS. PolicyCenter captures a signed acceptance before issuance — conduct and dispute exposure.

items.name: Instant issuance & document dispatch
items.detail: Policy issuance with 2-day document dispatch aligns with PolicyCenter issuance and fulfilment.

items.name: Renewal re-rating & retention
items.detail: Renewals trigger at 60 days but without retention/price-optimisation modelling or proactive win-back for non-take-up recommended by the catalog.

items.name: Producer / commission notification
items.detail: Automated commission statement and broker confirmation align with PolicyCenter producer management.

critical_missing.title: KYC / sanctions screening
critical_missing.detail: Applicants are not KYC-verified or screened against sanctions/PEP lists before binding, a regulatory control required by the catalog at new business.

critical_missing.title: Third-party risk-data enrichment
critical_missing.detail: Quotes rely on applicant-supplied data only, with no property/flood/credit/prior-claims enrichment — an anti-selection and pricing-accuracy gap.

critical_missing.title: Reinsurance / facultative referral
critical_missing.detail: No facultative reinsurance referral or treaty-capacity check for large commercial risks above retention, a capacity and accumulation-control gap.

critical_missing.title: Disclosure & e-signature acceptance
critical_missing.detail: Acceptance is confirmed only in CRM with no auditable signed acceptance of terms/PDS before issuance.

control_bars.name: Risk-data enrichment
control_bars.note: Applicant data only; no third-party enrichment

control_bars.name: Underwriting decisioning
control_bars.note: Referral, substandard terms and pricing solid

remediations.title: Add KYC / sanctions screening at new business
remediations.detail: Verify identity and screen applicants against sanctions/PEP lists before any bind; route hits to Compliance. Effort: 4 weeks.

remediations.title: Enrich submissions with third-party risk data
remediations.detail: Call property, flood, credit and prior-claims data at quote to improve pricing accuracy and control anti-selection. Effort: 5 weeks (data partners).

remediations.title: Introduce reinsurance facultative referral
remediations.detail: Add a treaty-capacity check and automatic facultative referral for risks above retention before binding large commercial business. Effort: 4 weeks.

remediations.title: Capture disclosure acknowledgement & e-signature
remediations.detail: Require an auditable signed acceptance of terms/PDS before issuance, stored against the policy. Effort: 3 weeks.

remediations.title: Add predictive scoring & renewal retention modelling
remediations.detail: Layer a predictive risk score over the rules engine and a retention/price-optimisation model on renewals with automated NTU win-back. Effort: 7 weeks.

opt_steps.output: Logged in CRM; prospect record created
Auditable needs analysis captured for conduct compliance
Property, flood, credit and prior-claims data appended before rating
Identity verified; applicant screened against sanctions/PEP lists
Rules plus predictive score widen straight-through acceptance
Risk assessed within 2–5 days; info requested if needed
Treaty-capacity check; facultative placement for risks above retention
Accept standard/substandard or decline; recorded in PAS
Acceptance letter or renewal notice; payment instructions
Auditable signed acceptance of terms/PDS captured before issuance
Policy number; coverage start date confirmed
Schedule, certificate and PDS within 2 business days
Commission statement issued; retention/price-optimisation flag set for renewal

opt_swim_phases.name: Prospect, quote & enrichment
opt_swim_phases.subtitle: Steps 1–5 · v3.0 adds risk-data enrichment
opt_swim_phases.note: v3.0 enriches every submission with third-party risk data before rating, improving pricing accuracy.

opt_swim_phases.name: Underwriting & screening
opt_swim_phases.subtitle: Steps 6–10 · screening, model, reinsurance
opt_swim_phases.note: v3.0 screens applicants before binding, scores them with a predictive model, and refers large risks to reinsurance.

opt_swim_phases.name: Acceptance & issuance
opt_swim_phases.subtitle: Steps 11–15 · v3.0 adds e-signature
opt_swim_phases.note: v3.0 captures an auditable e-signature acceptance before issuance and tags renewals for retention modelling.

optimised_doc.subtitle: Optimised per Fit-Gap Analysis against the Guidewire PolicyCenter Underwriting Catalog

blocks: OPTIMISATION SUMMARY — What changed in v3.0

This version incorporates 5 remediation actions from Fit-Gap Report FG-INS-004. Alignment to the Guidewire PolicyCenter underwriting catalog increased from 69% to 91%. Critical additions: KYC/sanctions screening, third-party risk-data enrichment, reinsurance facultative referral, disclosure e-signature, predictive risk scoring, and renewal retention modelling.
NEW STEP (green) — step added to close a gap vs the PolicyCenter catalog
ENHANCED (amber) — existing step updated with additional controls or automation

sections.title: 1. Purpose
blocks: This Standard Operating Procedure defines the end-to-end process from prospect enquiry to policy issuance for new business and renewals. Version 3.0 incorporates optimisations from fit-gap analysis against the Guidewire PolicyCenter New Business & Renewal Underwriting Catalog, achieving 91% alignment (up from 69% in v2.1).

sections.title: 2. Scope
blocks: This procedure applies to new business across personal lines and commercial SME products and to renewals, received via online, agent, broker and bancassurance channels.
Scope extended: KYC/sanctions screening and risk-data enrichment now apply to every application before binding.
This procedure does NOT cover claims-triggered reinstatements, group enrolments (SOP-INS-006), or life and health products.

sections.title: 4. Roles and Responsibilities
blocks: Captures enquiries and needs analysis; initiates applications; communicates terms; coordinates to issuance.
Reviews referred applications; sets terms and pricing; approves/declines; manages reinsurance referral.
Maintains rating and models; prices non-standard risk; owns renewal retention modelling.
Owns KYC/sanctions dispositions and conduct/suitability governance.

sections.title: 5. Screening & Risk Data (New Section — v3.0)
blocks: New section. Required by the PolicyCenter catalog. KYC/sanctions screening and risk-data enrichment must complete before any policy is bound.

sections.title: 7. Exception Handling
blocks: Application status set to 'Not Taken Up' in PAS with a reason code.
CRM follow-up task set for 14 days; an automated win-back offer is sent where pricing flexibility exists.
If no response within 21 days of expiry, a final reminder is sent.
Policy lapses on expiry if no renewal payment received; reinstatement per SOP-INS-011.
Applicant notified in writing within 1 business day with reasons permitted by guidelines and regulation.
Applicant referred to an alternative market where a known placement pathway exists.
New exception. Applications failing KYC or hitting a sanctions match are held pending Compliance review.
KYC failure: document-upload fallback offered; unresolved cases referred to Underwriting.
Sanctions/PEP hit: application held; Compliance dispositions within 1 business day before any bind.

sections.title: 8. Systems and Tools
blocks: Premium calculation, auto-accept rules and predictive scoring
Property, flood, credit and prior-claims enrichment

sections.title: 9. Key Controls and Audit Points
blocks: Controls expanded in v3.0: KYC/sanctions screening, risk-data enrichment, reinsurance referral, e-signature acceptance and predictive scoring.
Screening gate: no bind without KYC and sanctions clearance.
Risk-data enrichment: third-party data appended to every submission before rating.
Capacity control: large risks referred to reinsurance with a treaty-capacity check.
Disclosure capture: auditable e-signature on terms/PDS before issuance.
Predictive scoring: rules-engine acceptance refined by a governed risk model.
Suitability: structured needs analysis recorded for conduct compliance.
Immutable audit trail: enquiry, screening, decision, acceptance and issuance events logged.

sections.title: 11. Document Control
blocks: Updated underwriting SLAs; added broker portal steps; revised retention KPIs
OPTIMISED: KYC/sanctions screening; risk-data enrichment; reinsurance facultative referral; e-signature acceptance; predictive scoring; renewal retention modelling; catalog alignment 69% → 91%
